campaigns.name: Planning Analytics — New Whitespace Florida
campaigns.tagline: Move from reactive planning to proactive, insight-driven decision-making.

emails.subject: Your forecast cycle — quick question
emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

Most finance teams I speak with are still manually rebuilding forecasts every time conditions shift.

IBM Planning Analytics runs unlimited what-if scenarios in real time and updates financial models automatically as inputs change — no data science team required.

Worth 20 minutes to see how it applies to [Company]?

Bradley Milks
Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning | IBM | Florida

emails.subject: Ask your plan a question
emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

Following up from last week.

Planning Analytics now lets your team interact with planning data in plain language — ask a question, get instant insights, auto-generate reports and narratives.

Finance, ops, and business teams all working from one model. No analyst bottleneck.

Open to a quick look?

Bradley Milks
Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning | IBM | Florida

emails.subject: Planning that runs itself
emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

One more thought on Planning Analytics.

The newest capability: AI agents that continuously monitor your plan, flag variances, and trigger alerts — without anyone pulling a report.

Closed-loop planning. Your team focuses on decisions, not data prep.

15 minutes to see it?

Bradley Milks
Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning | IBM | Florida

emails.subject: Last note — IBM Planning Analytics
emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

Didn't want to keep reaching out without checking in one last time.

Three things Planning Analytics delivers: better forecast accuracy, broader team participation, and less manual work.

If any of that is on your radar for [Company] this year, I'd love to connect.

Bradley Milks
Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning | IBM | Florida

campaigns.name: Planning Analytics — Expansion (Existing IBM)
campaigns.tagline: Your team is already on Planning Analytics — here's what AI unlocks next.

emails.subject: What's new in Planning Analytics
emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

Since [Company] is already on Planning Analytics, wanted to flag what's new.

The AI layer now runs real-time what-if scenarios and incorporates external drivers automatically — adaptive learning that continuously improves forecast accuracy.

Most teams using these features have cut their forecast cycle significantly.

Worth a quick sync to walk through what's available to you?

Bradley Milks
Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning | IBM | Florida

emails.subject: Natural language planning — PA update
emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

Following up on Planning Analytics.

New generative AI capability: your team can ask questions of your planning model in plain language and get instant answers, reports, and narratives.

Broader participation across finance, ops, and business — without adding headcount.

Open to 20 minutes?

Bradley Milks
Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning | IBM | Florida

emails.subject: Autonomous planning — quick look?
emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

Last note on Planning Analytics upgrades.

Agentic AI: AI agents now monitor your plan continuously, automate repetitive workflows, and recommend actions — all without manual intervention.
